seoTitle: Set Up Trailing Stop Loss on TradingView (Fix Missing Button)
metaDescription: Learn how to enable trailing stop loss on TradingView's order panel and fix it when the trailing SL button isn't showing on your broker.
intro: Trailing Stop Loss is a powerful tool for locking in profits while letting winning trades run, but setting it up correctly on TradingView means using the right broker connection and order panel settings. This guide covers how to enable and adjust trailing stops through TradingView's integrated broker panel, plus what to do if the trailing SL button isn't showing at all. It works whether you're trading through connected accounts like OANDA, FXCM, or another supported broker.

steps.heading: Understand how trailing stop loss works
steps.body: A trailing stop loss automatically adjusts as a trade moves in your favor, helping lock in gains without manually updating your stop level.

steps.heading: Open the TradingView order panel and locate the trailing SL feature
steps.body: The trailing stop setting lives inside TradingView's order panel, connected to your broker account, so start there to find the option.

steps.heading: Set the distance and activate trailing stop loss
steps.body: Adjust the trailing distance in pips or percentage terms and activate the feature so it starts trailing your open position.

steps.heading: Fix the trailing SL button not showing
steps.body: If the button is missing, common causes include using a demo account, connecting to an unsupported broker, or having an inactive order type — adjusting your order preferences or broker connection resolves this.

steps.heading: Apply tips for managing trades safely
steps.body: Use sensible trailing distances to avoid premature exits during volatile price swings, and monitor the trade on desktop or mobile as needed.

faq.question: Why can't I see the trailing stop loss button on TradingView?
faq.answer: This is often due to using a demo account, connecting to a broker that doesn't support trailing stops, or having an inactive order type selected.

faq.question: Does trailing stop loss work on all brokers connected to TradingView?
faq.answer: No, trailing stop support depends on the broker — accounts like OANDA and FXCM are mentioned as supported, but not every connected broker offers it.

faq.question: Can I use trailing stop loss on TradingView mobile?
faq.answer: Yes, trailing stop loss can be activated, adjusted, and monitored on both desktop and mobile versions of TradingView.

faq.question: How do I set the trailing distance?
faq.answer: You can set the trailing distance in either pips or percentage terms directly within the order panel when activating the trailing stop.

faq.question: Can a trailing stop loss exit my trade too early?
faq.answer: Yes, setting the trailing distance too tight can cause premature exits during volatile price movements, so choosing a sensible distance matters.
